CREATE TABLE soap_requests (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, request_body TEXT, encryption_status TEXT);

WITH encrypted_request AS (SELECT xenc_encrypt(request_body, 11, dsig_template_ext(...), vector(), '/Envelope/Body', xenc_key_inst_create('myAES', xenc_key_inst_create('rsa-test')), 'Content') AS encrypted_body FROM soap_requests WHERE encryption_status = 'unencrypted') SELECT * FROM encrypted_request;
UPDATE soap_requests SET encryption_status = 'encrypted' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, request_body, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM soap_requests WHERE encryption_status = 'encrypted') AS encrypted_count FROM soap_requests;

SELECT r.id, r.request_body, xenc_encrypt(r.request_body, 11, dsig_template_ext(...), vector(), '/Envelope/Body', xenc_key_inst_create('myAES', xenc_key_inst_create('rsa-test')), 'Content') AS encrypted_body FROM soap_requests r WHERE r.encryption_status = 'unencrypted' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM soap_requests WHERE encryption_status = 'encrypted');
SELECT r.id, r.request_body FROM soap_requests r JOIN (SELECT id FROM soap_requests WHERE encryption_status = 'unencrypted') AS u ON r.id = u.id;
DELETE FROM soap_requests WHERE encryption_status = 'encrypted' AND id IN (SELECT id FROM soap_requests WHERE request_body IS NOT NULL);
